title: CPR — Child/Infant (Under 12)

steps.instruction: Check for responsiveness. Tap the child's foot (infant) or shoulder (child) and shout.
steps.tip: Never shake a baby. Flick the sole of the foot gently for infants.

steps.instruction: Give 5 initial rescue breaths. Cover the infant's mouth AND nose with your mouth. For children, pinch nose and cover mouth. Blow gently for 1 second each.
steps.tip: Use gentle puffs for infants — their lungs are tiny. Watch for chest rise.

steps.instruction: For INFANTS: Use 2 fingers on the center of the chest, just below the nipple line. Compress 1.5 inches deep. For CHILDREN: Use 1 hand heel on center of chest. Compress 2 inches deep.
steps.tip: Rate: 100-120 compressions per minute for both.

steps.instruction: Give 30 compressions followed by 2 rescue breaths. Continue this cycle.
steps.tip: For infants, small puffs only. Over-inflation can cause lung damage.

steps.instruction: After 2 minutes of CPR (about 5 cycles), call emergency services if not already done. Then continue CPR.
steps.tip: If alone with an infant, carry them while doing CPR to reach a phone.

steps.instruction: Continue CPR until emergency services arrive, the child starts breathing, or an AED arrives.
steps.tip: Child AED pads should be used if available. If not, adult pads can be used — place one on front, one on back.

do_not: Do NOT use adult compression depth on infants
Do NOT blow too hard — gentle puffs for infants
Do NOT delay — every second counts
